description_long: This structure is a large, circular triumphal arch composed of a ring-shaped entablature supported by evenly spaced, tall columns. The columns are fluted and appear to be made of stone, reminiscent of classical Greco-Roman architecture. The open center and absence of a roof or connecting arches between columns give it a monumental, pavilion-like appearance. The stonework is weathered, with a grayish tone and subtle textural variations, suggesting age and durability. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels on the entablature or columns. The overall design is simple yet grand, evoking the style of ancient Roman monuments such as the Temple of Vesta or a peristyle rotunda.
description: A circular stone triumphal arch with fluted columns and a ring-shaped entablature, evoking classical Roman architecture. The structure is open in the center and lacks decorative reliefs or inscription panels.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full circular arrangement of columns and the continuous entablature, highlighting the open center.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the depth and spacing of the columns, with the entablature's thickness more apparent.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the uniformity of the columns and the shadowed interior, with no additional features visible.
description_view_3: This angle provides a clear look at the column alignment and the smooth, unadorned entablature.
